context: Defines zero-knowledge bootstrap protocol. AODS v3 adds layer-aware progressive disclosure: root first, capsule next, detail on demand, evidence only on explicit review or debugging paths. Large corpora may also expose boot_by_touch for file-scoped authoring routes, manifest runtime-contract summaries for risk-aware loading, task-stage-aware query routing, lexical query routing for grep-compatible discovery, and minimal JSON route explanations.
assumptions: Agent has filesystem access to corpus root directory
Agent has finite context window with known token budget

sections.content: Read manifest.json. Choose boot list: boot_by_role[role_id] if role assigned else boot_sequence. companion.roles is runtime profile only: id + capability hints, not duplicated boot bindings when boot_by_role already exists. Load root modules first. If budget remains and task needs routing summary -> load capsule module. Load detail modules only when root+capsule insufficient. Load evidence only for explicit review, debugging, or proof tasks.

Read manifest.json. Compare manifest.sys_v against cached sys_v. If equal -> resume. If different -> diff changed modules. breaking=true -> full reload of affected module plus dependents. breaking=false -> read delta plus affected artifacts only. If changed module.layer=root|capsule -> refresh routing cache before task resume.

sections.topic: context window budget management during boot
sections.content: Reserve task budget first. Use modules[].tokens_approx + modules[].priority + modules[].layer to schedule loads. Prefer root over capsule over detail over evidence when budget pressure exists. Evidence layer is first eviction target. Detail is second. Root stays pinned while task active.

If task is anchored to one touched file and manifest.boot_by_touch contains matching route -> prefer route.load_modules over broad role boot or query routing. Exact path beats glob. Higher-specificity glob beats lower-specificity glob. Always include touched module itself when touched path equals modules[].path. If a paired human_primary and its paired agent_primary both declare exact touch routes for the same intent, keep the human route inside the paired agent route and include the pair's semantic modules in the human route. Use boot_by_role, query routing, or boot_sequence only when no touch route exists.

manifest.modules[].runtime_contract is bootstrap summary: side_effects + outputs only. module.meta.runtime_contract carries full execution contract: tools, resources, telemetry, checkpoints, outputs. Read the manifest summary before choosing an execution path with external effects. Open module.meta.runtime_contract when the task needs exact environment assumptions, visible telemetry, or resumable checkpoints. If side_effects != ['none'] -> do not execute blindly; require at least one observable anchor from telemetry|checkpoints|outputs.

route CLI may accept --stage orientation|plan|action|verification|evidence. If stage is absent, query routing may infer it from lexical cues. Stage refines ranking inside the current layer model instead of replacing layers: orientation prefers root|capsule, plan prefers architecture|policy detail, action prefers workflow|policy detail, verification prefers review or sensor-capable modules, evidence prefers evidence-layer or audit-heavy references. Use stage when the task goal is clearer than the exact file target. route --json exposes explanation.source, explanation.reason, and explanation.dependency so agents can inspect why the working set was selected without parsing text output.

sections.topic: lazy module loading during task execution
sections.content: If no touch route wins, extract task query terms and match them against stable lexical anchors such as module_id, scope, tags, section topics, artifact IDs, artifact usage, compact artifact notes, capsule hints, paired human paths, and declared shared invariants. Modules may be section-first or artifact-first, so routing must index both short prose fields and structured artifact semantics. If role assigned -> prefer matches inside that role's boot_by_role component unless stronger cross-component evidence exists. Expand explicit aliases only when they are deterministic and grep-compatible. Use layer as a routing preference: root for orientation, capsule for summary, detail for semantic authority, evidence for proof only. If touch path exists and boot_by_touch route matches -> load route modules before lexical query routing. Load deps before target only when the task requires edit or execution context; do not expand read queries into broad dependency closure by default. Human surfaces never replace agent-primary semantic source; use manifest.surface_pairs only to locate paired human narrative when needed.

ref=sid -> resolve in current module. ref=module_id:sid -> load target module if needed, then resolve section. meta.capsule.routes_to may contain module_id or module_id:sid; resolve same way. Missing target -> warning. Circular refs allowed; visited set required.

artifacts.usage: Canonical boot workflow for AODS v3 readers.

steps.action: load candidate_modules where layer=root in listed order
load best-fit capsule module from boot list or semantic match
load best-match detail modules via scope|tags|deps
load evidence modules only for explicit validation or adjudication
begin task execution with active working set

artifacts.usage: Select next layer during task-time loading.

nodes.action: load root-layer module from boot list
load detail module matched by scope|tags; detail wins semantic conflicts
load evidence module only after authority module already loaded
check manifest.surface_pairs; keep agent-primary module as semantic source

artifacts.usage: Lookup table for load tier, budget priority, and eviction order.
Lookup table for when boot_by_touch should override broader boot lists.

rows: single known file is being edited
minimize loaded rules to the touched authoring surface
role known but no file-specific route exists
no role and no file-specific route exist

artifacts.usage: Field guide for manifest summary vs full module runtime contract.

rows: risk-aware routing without loading full detail
full environment contract and observability anchors

artifacts.usage: Field guide for task-stage-aware query routing.

rows: What should happen and in what order?
What do I change or execute now?
How do I prove or check this?
kind, role, touch, query, stage, stage_source, intent
captures the route input and route strategy that produced the working set
strategy, fallback, selected_module_ids, matched_touch_routes, matched_query_module_ids, touched_module, touched_surface_pair, text
lets agents consume selected modules and match source without scraping prose
source, mode, selected_module_ids, dependency_ids, selected_dependency_ids, unselected_dependency_ids, edges
shows declared module dependency edges that affected or contextualize the selected working set

changelog.delta: Added layer-aware progressive disclosure, capsule loading, evidence gate, and human-surface lookup rules.
Added explicit touch-routing precedence for file-scoped authoring flows.
Clarified that non-touch routing should be lexical-plus-structural, grep-compatible, and dependency-light by default.
Added runtime contract summaries and observability guidance so bootstrap can reason about execution risk before loading full detail modules.
Added task-stage-aware query routing so stage intent can refine module ranking beyond layer-only disclosure.
Added route subcommand discoverability expectations for CLI help without changing route ranking.
Added minimal route JSON explanation envelope for source, reason, and dependency metadata.
